programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs must cook their fish courses but with some exceptionally high scoring dishes in the regional heats, each chef will need to deliver perfection in order to see off the competition. The judges are joined by guest judge Tim Henman.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day and with only two spots remaining on the banquet menu, the competition is fierce. As the barbecues are fired up, all 8 chefs are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by guest judgeand Paralympic gold medallist, Gordon Reid.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is dessert day and with just one place remaining at the Wimbledon banquet, each chef is giving everything they have got. The judges are joined by guest judge Annabel Croft, former British Number One and Junior Wimbledon Champion.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the grand finale and the winning chefs must now cook their dishes at the banquet. Once in the kitchen, the chefs face the huge challenge of scaling up their dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Fridays judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the regions chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll, which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industrys finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: For his main course, Ryan Simpson-Trotman is confident his dish, `The UKs Number 1, will hit the top spot with his combination of a beef pie and rump cap. Sabrina Gidda is taking inspiration from her grandads collection of old 45s for her `Sunday Jukebox main: pork Wellington and Black Country faggots.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central chefs must cook their four course menus again for judges food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Competing for a place on the banquet menu are two chefs returning to the competition for a second time, plus a supremely talented newcomer. Liam Simpson, who co-owns a high-end gastropub near Henley-on-Thames, is hoping to make it all the way after being knocked out in the heats during the 2018 competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through the regional heats and, as the lowest scoring chef will be eliminated from the competition at the end of the show, the pressure is on. They start by cooking their all-important main courses, the centrepiece of the banquet menu. Michelin-starred newcomer Hrishikesh Desai is pushing himself to the limit with a complex multi-part venison dish, inspired by the bands which play at the Kendal Calling music festival.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs from the north west cook their dishes head to head for a panel of expert judges, in a bid to secure themselves a place in the national finals. Will they have acted on the advice given by the veteran judge earlier in the week, and learn from their mistakes?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands finest chefs do battle for a chance to cook at the banquet celebrating 50 Years of Popular Music. Gordon Jones, the only newcomer, faces returners Lorna McNee and Ben Reade, who are going head-to-head for the second time.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their mains and desserts, each battling to produce smash hit dishes that wow their veteran. For her main, Lorna is confident her lamb dish `With A Little Help From My Friends, dedicated to supportive friends, is a celebratory dish fit for the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs must cook their four courses again, but this time theyll need to appeal to four new palates: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton, broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver plus a celebrity guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Emily Scott is the chef proprietor of Cornwalls renowned `St Tudy Inn. Her two rivals are from across the sea on Jersey. Joe Baker is largely self-taught, but at just 28 already running his own restaurant, `No 10, in St Helier. A neighbouring restaurant is Michelin starred `Samphire where Lee Smith is head chef.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores the aftermath of WWII in the wake of destruction and liberation, with tens of millions dead and millions more displaced.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives
programme.desc: The Nazi regime lasted from 1933 to 1945 and was one of the most horrific periods of history. However, Adolf Hitler was not working alone. He had a circle of some of the most barbaric men alongside him, who helped make the atrocities possible.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: What persuaded one-time "Man of the Year" Pierre Laval to send Jews to the death camps?

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Uncovers the motives behind the people and organisations who made the choice to aid the Nazi war effort.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Viktor Arajs formed a Latvian commando unit that hunted Jews.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: If Leon Degrelle, a writer and journalist, had lived in another era, then he could have been a hero and a leader. However, he became a supporter of Hitler and ended up betraying his faith, his country and his people.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Croat Dinko Sakic ran an extermination camp in Axis-occupied Yugoslavia.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: The story of Vidkun Quisling, who declared himself Fuhrer of occupied Norway.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: While the IRA never shared fascist ideology, a number of members traded intelligence with Abwehr as they sought an end to British rule in Ireland.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Historians are divided over Mohammad Amin al-Husayni, Grand Mufti of Jerusalem during WWII. While some say he was unfairly demonised, others point to his ties with fascist Europe.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.sub-title: The Jew Who Fought for Hitler
programme.desc: The 1935 Nuremburg Laws laid the groundwork for what would evolve into The Final Solution.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Anton Mussert was one of the founders of the National Socialist Movement in the Netherlands and worked with the Gestapo.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Greece had a well integrated Jewish community and lost 300,000 citizens thanks to Nazi occupiers; many Greeks collaborated with the Nazis.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Scrutinising the motives behind the individuals and groups who aided the Nazi war effort. Respected mathematician Rolf Nevanlinna led the Finnish SS against Stalins Russia.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: The Schutzmannschaft were the collaborationist auxiliary police forces of Nazi-occupied countries in Eastern Europe.

programme.title: Operation Carpetbagger: The Secret Mission to End World War II
programme.desc: Chronicling Operation Carpetbagger, one of the most top-secret missions of World War II.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Many mysteries remain surrounding the establishment of the Mayan city in Chichen Itzá. How was it constructed?

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Experts explore the ancient Mayan city of Tikal and ask why it was abandoned despite its dominance.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Teotihuacan was discovered by Aztecs in the 14th century. But who built such magnificent structures as these?

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: The truth about the pharaoh who amended Egyptian religion to monotheism.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: We join a team as they undertake the largest maritime investigation since the Mary Rose and reveal the extraordinary story of HMS Invincible.

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage
programme.desc: A veteran comes to the rescue for a crew that has lost a valuable clam dredge in stormy waters. Unable to fish without it, their livelihoods are at stake.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: After seizing power in 1933, Adolf Hitler began systematically transforming a peaceful German population into a ruthless war machine. Experts investigate the innovative methods used by the Nazis to indoctrinate and control the will of its citizens.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Historian Dan Jones is in the heart of Snowdonia exploring a stronghold built not only as an impenetrable fortress but also as a lavish royal palace fit for a fairytale - Caernarfon Castle.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The quest continues in the Red Sea off the coast of Egypt, a deeply personal journey given Ross grandfather fought in the North Africa campaign during World War II.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Inspired by the story of his two cousins that died at sea in WWII, Ross Kemp explores captured German scout warship, the F2 and brings up treasure that could link back to his relatives.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: The Kaiser Sunken Fleet: Ross Kemp continues his adventure in Scapa Flow, plunging down on the wrecks of a German warship and an experimental submarine aircraft called the M2. (S1, ep 2)

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross Kemp dives down on shipwrecks to uncover hidden stories, and recover treasure. He dives on warships, experimental submarines, early slaver ships and Napoleonic frigates, searching for clues and artefacts that reveal the story behind every wreck.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: After hearing about his great-grandfather who served in the Merchant Navy, Ross Kemp is inspired to explore the wreck of a WWII cargo ship and try to recover its military supplies.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross heads to Portsmouth and the Mary Rose Museum and takes up an opportunity he cant turn down, to be one of the few divers allowed on the site of Britains most famous wreck, the Mary Rose.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Tunstall in Shropshire and Prescott in Liverpool and a large flat in Dagenham are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion have a look round before hearing what the new owners have planned.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are todays investors. Theyve bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts rolls back the clock nearly 12 years to catch up on an ambitious development of a former church in Preston on the Hill in Cheshire, while Lucy Alexander looks round two very different terraced houses in Stoney Stratford, near Milton Keynes, and Streatham in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s terrace in Croydon, a semi in Wolstanton destined to be a family home and the only house on a street in Moorside in Oldham are all under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion visit the properties and meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in Londons Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in Londons Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin tackles the issue of solar panels on a property in Mountain Ash in Wales and wonders if itll be a sunny outcome for the new owner, while Lucy looks round a barn in Elstree in Hertfordshire and Dion visits a flat in Newport in Shropshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets a couple who buy a former millworkers cottage in Derbyshire as their first home together; Lucy Alexander meets a father and daughter team who are seasoned developers buying a house in Kent; and Dion Dublin talks to a man who, along with two friends, has clubbed together to buy an investment property in Lowestoft in Suffolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin is in Walsall in the Midlands looking around a 1950s semi; Martin Roberts meets a man developing a ground-floor one-bedroom flat in Ilford in Essex; and Lucy Alexander catches up on a multi-million-pound redevelopment in East Horsley in Surrey where one small cottage with a big garden is converted into eight separate properties.

programme.desc: In Snellville, Georgia, Heather Strube has a great life of working in the family floral shop, raising her toddler son, and looking forward to a bright future with her new boyfriend, until a Sunday trip to the shopping centre shatters everything.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: 14-year-old Ashley Summers vanishes in 2007. The mystery deepens when the FBI connects her case to two other young women missing from her neighbourhood.

programme.title: Disappeared
programme.desc: 19-year-old Logan Schiendelman vanishes after a night out. When authorities find no evidence of foul play, they are forced to examine his familys controversial past.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death
programme.desc: In 1993, a family is torn apart when a mother in Jacksonville, Florida goes missing. The case stumps detectives, who believe that the truth about what happened to her is locked inside the mind of the missing womans three-year-old son.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here: Shadows of Death
programme.desc: In 1996, in Tulsa, Oklahoma, a young mother is found dead on the side of a rural stretch of motorway in an apparent hit-and-run accident. But as the family grieves, the façade begins to crack, and soon the truth seeps out.

programme.title: Mean Girl Murders
programme.desc: At a Warren, Michigan, high school, its the smart, ambitious kids who rule the school. Two of the best and brightest are seniors, Danyna Gibson and Tanaya Lewis. And when they compete for more than just grades, the results turn deadly.

programme.title: The Murder of Sarah Everard: A Faking It Special
programme.desc: A tragic story of a brutal murder and a killer cop. Analysing CCTV, police bodycam and interview tapes, experts unpick the behaviour of notorious killer Wayne Couzens, who murdered 33 year old Sarah Everard in a crime that shocked the nation.

programme.title: A Killer Among Friends
programme.desc: When a California teen disappears, the whole town feels on edge. As suspicion turns inward, her closest friends begin to wonder if the danger was beside them all along, hidden in the familiar faces of classmates with something dark to hide.

programme.title: Murder Under the Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: The Hill family lives in a gated community near Dawson HS, where their daughter Arrijana is a popular athlete. When she is found murdered in her bedroom, detectives learn that Arrijana was hiding a secret that leads them to her killer.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: Elishabas father tormented and abused her for decades until she has enough and embarks on a daring escape through the Alaskan wilderness.

programme.title: Evil Lives Here
programme.desc: LaTonya believes her husband, Nathan, is her best friend. Over the years she suffers emotional abuse at his hands and decides to take their kids and leave. Nathan refuses to let go, leading to a night of terror that ends in a tragedy - and a miracle.

programme.title: Evil Online
programme.desc: Musician Ray Merrill thinks he has found his dream woman in Brazil through a dating website. However, as the exotic romance heats up, money starts to go missing.

programme.title: Evil Online
programme.desc: Nicole White disappears in the middle of a bitter custody battle. Suspicion falls on and online date, but could her husband be responsible for her disappearance?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: The Summit
programme.desc: The final four make the last terrifying ascent to The Summit and, as ever, there are obstacles in the way that include a dangerous crevasse crossing which sees one of them frozen in fear and anothers attempt made harder by the menacing Mountains Keeper - with their eyes on the prize, the four are tested physically and emotionally as they are forced to make a devastating decision at the last-ever vote-off, but the final ascent is put in jeopardy by bad weather.

programme.title: Unwind with ITV
programme.desc: A daily delivery of bespoke Mindfulness content designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.

programme.title: Ant and Decs Limitless Win
programme.desc: Brothers Alistair and Ashley - and aunt and nephew Denise and Pablo - play the game.

programme.title: Celebrity Lingo
programme.desc: Adil Ray hosts the game of words as `Emmerdale icons Lisa Riley and Mark Charnock take on the Reverend Richard Coles and comedian Tim Vine to win big for charity.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team demonstrate how to bring a woodland into your garden as they create a cross between a woodland glade and a forest school for care worker Carl and his family. Inspired by Japanese `forest bathing, woodland dens, natural play, dramatic trees and undergrowth teeming with wildlife, Alan, Katie Rushworth and Danny Clarke transform a Macclesfield back garden into a slice of nature, complete with family seating, a woodland Wendy-house and a lift for wife Elles wheelchair.

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Joining Alan at Manor Farm are actor James Murray and Lynda La Plante to provide the chat - florist Jonathan Moseley is here with innovative ideas for Mothering Sunday, we meet one of the rarest sheep breeds in the UK and welcome their lambs, and mums the word with the Best of British feature as Andy Clarke serves up his Mothers Day martinis.

programme.title: James Martins Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James welcomes singer Alfie Boe and Loose Womens Penny Lancaster to his home in the Hampshire countryside while also tasting delicious recipes from chefs Paul Rankin and Shivi Ramoutar.

programme.title: EFL LIVE
programme.desc: The race for promotion to the Premier League is hotting up and Ipswich Town and Millwall are both in the running. Ipswich Town were relegated from the top division last season, and Kieran McKenna is looking to take them straight back up, whilst Millwall have not been in the top flight since the 1990s. Hugh Woozencroft presents coverage of this key Championship clash from Portman Road alongside Darren Bent and Tony Cascarino. Sam Matterface and Iain Dowie provide commentary.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news.

programme.title: Julia Bradburys Wonders of the Frozen South
programme.desc: Julia Bradbury sails to the rugged island of South Georgia - rising from the South Atlantic like a lost world with staggering natural beauty, it has an extraordinary abundance of wildlife and haunting human history. Landing on Salisbury Plain, home to one of the greatest wildlife spectacles on the planet, Julia navigates past aggressive fur seals that patrol the shoreline to reach a vast colony of over 100,000 king penguins that crowd the beach, a true wonder of the frozen south.

programme.desc: The Bodmin community is shocked when unexplained killings begin to occur. Frightened by these unexplained tragedies, the locals begin to believe a local myth has reared its ugly head.

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: On Halloween night, a university friend of pathologist Laura Hobson is found staked through the heart. During their investigation, Lewis and Hathaway are approached by a medium who claims that they are in grave danger. As the body count rises Lewis and Hathaway are forced to acknowledge that Hobson might know more about the deaths than she is letting on.

programme.title: Agatha Christies Marple
programme.desc: When a young blonde woman is found strangled in the library of Gossington Hall, Dolly Bantry quickly calls in her old friend Miss Marple to help her with a bit of sleuthing. They soon discover that the dead woman was a dancer from the glamorous Majestic Hotel and decide to check themselves in there in order to observe the guests and solve the murder.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: As Laura and Rosemary visit a prep school to investigate dying roses, they are caught up in a strange ritual involving a member of staff dressed as a stag. The geography master is then found in the garden with a harpoon through his chest.

programme.title: Rosemary and Thyme
programme.desc: Rosemary and Laura enter the cut-throat world of TV garden makeovers when they help a presenter with her new series. When Rosemary is injured in a suspicious accident, Laura and the presenter team up to investigate further.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The annual Midsomer Regatta is brought to a standstill when a body is found in the river. It turns out to be that of a noted ladies man who had been previously seen arguing with friends. As Barnaby and Scott dig into the luxury lifestyles of the rowing set, they discover that money problems and sexual jealousy are rife.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When one of the worlds rarest orchids is smuggled illegally into Midsomer Malham, it triggers a catalogue of passion, jealousy and death. Barnaby and Troy must find both the flower and the person prepared to commit murder in order to get their hands on it.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate a death in Midsomer Mere, where the villagers appear to be able to predict the future. As the investigation continues, further deaths occur and Barnaby must delve into the villages history for clues to solve the murders.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a solicitor falls from a roof to his death, Barnaby and Scott investigate whether it was suicide or murder. They discover that a rival lawyer was spreading harmful rumours about the dead man, but when this man is drugged and killed as well, the motive looks like revenge - but for what?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At Village Limits in Woodhall Spa, Lincolnshire, David and his best friend Kim hope their quirky, stylish rooms will propel them to victory, but theres cheeky chat from one guest and some bathroom issues.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At the The Old Mill in Yarm, mum-and-daughter duo Zoe and Mea hope to win their guests over with the life of luxury. But two guests think the extra cost for a deluxe room is a bit rich.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Elan Hotel in Powys, Martin and Iris offer a no-frills approach and a banging breakfast. But the rooms lack the wow factor, and some of the feedback scores prove hard for the hosts to digest.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests visit The Royal Oak Inn in Somerset, run by Spencer and Gavin. The hosts have talked a good game all week, so the guests arrive at the 14th-century inn with high expectations, but are shocked to find the standards to be rather, standard.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guests rifle through their feedback, from troubles over a breakfast hamper to dodgy decor decisions. After strong opinions are aired and differences debated, the payments are revealed and the winner is announced.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mum and daughter Maria and Jess look for their dream holiday home on Portugals Silver Coast for £130k. Will any of Ben Hillmans options be right up their street?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Chris and Jane are planning a permanent move to Lot-et-Garonne in the southwest of France with their dog, Loki. Danni Menzies shows them what their £200k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Clergyman Ken is planning a permanent move to Larnaca in Cyprus, so he can be closer to his friends. With a budget of £80,000, can Jasmine Harman find the answer to his prayers?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition takes place in and around Leeds, where the first to host is 24-year-old digital account manager Alice. Alice has never held a dinner party before, but shes hoping her family favourites will hit the spot and win her the grand. Shes hoping to impress Danielle, Andy, Lynne and Matt. With gob-smacking revelations, cringe-worthy party tricks and a huge Pavlova, can Alice walk away with the prize?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its night two of the competition in Leeds, and company director Andy is hoping to win the grand with a menu of fancy restaurant inspired fare. But Alice and Danielle are more impressed with Andys Ferrari than his food. And Lynne is more concerned with turning out a perfect Mojito in the cocktail making challenge than anything else. Will Andys posh nosh be to everyones taste, and can he hold his composure till the end of the night to bag top spot?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third night of the competition in Leeds, and fun-loving student Danielle is hoping her hearty and high calorie menu will bag her the prize. The laughs come thick and fast for sports car fanatic Andy, while account manager Alice finds herself in food heaven. But when Danielle serves dessert in her wedding dress, lighting technician Matt is prompted to propose to sales consultant Lynne and fails spectacularly.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in Leeds this week is theatre technician Matt, whos planning to scoop the prize with a menu of tried and tested family favourites. The food is a winner for student Danielle and shes even more impressed by his juggling skills. But neither hit the mark for IT director Andy, who declares it the worst night of the week so far and things go from bad to worse for Matt when Alices dessert ends up on the floor.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition in Leeds, and jeweller Lynne is hoping her night will sparkle with a menu of made of her own creations. While talk at the table turns to plastic surgery and Botox, Andy and Danielle arent too impressed with Lynnes food. Can she rescue her dinner party with a memorable karaoke performance and sing her way to top spot and bag the thousand pounds?

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nyoami and Joe host first at The Trap Inn in Northumberland. The guests love the decor, and a boat trip to spot some puffins is a big hit. But their porridge is a real sticking point...

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: At The Newholme Hotel in Blackpool, Nigel and Damian offer budget prices with high-end touches. But a surprise in the loo and a slimy serving at breakfast cause serious concerns.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Liz and Tracey host at Portsonachan Hotel in Argyll and Bute. Some dirty discoveries dampen the guests initial delight, and a guided tour of Inveraray Castle serves up some spooky tales.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Lamlash Bay Hotel on the Isle of Arran. As former cabin crew, host Meg hopes her customer service will fly her to victory, but a follicle failure taints her feedback.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: On the final day, the guests rifle through the feedback, which covers a lavatory let-down and fiddled French toast. The payments are revealed and a winner is announced.

programme.desc: Adam, whos 20, is brought in by ambulance after being involved in a high-speed motorcycle accident. Hes joined by his dad and sister.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Forty-seven-year-old Gary was on his motorcycle when he had a head-on collision with a car. The single father of two is a code red trauma and is flown 50 miles to Kings by air ambulance.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Scaffolder Thomas has been struck on the back of the head by a six-metre pole at a building site in South London. He lost consciousness and was convulsing at the scene. Meanwhile, Hilton has diabetes and is worried about his swollen legs, and retired architect Edgar comes in after suffering an asthma attack.

programme.desc: Paramedics treat a man having an anaphylactic reaction, a patient whose having a heart attack and a man receiving chemotherapy who may have developed sepsis.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a childs chair, a musical wedding album and a mechanical, camel-shaped childrens ride weathered by a lifetime spent beside the sea.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Soft toy restorers Julie Tatchell and Amanda Middleditch are entrusted to revive a worn-out rabbit belonging to Liz Warne. As a baby Liz suffered from a lung condition which left doctors telling her parents she wouldnt survive. Hospitalised for a long time, they could only give little Liz a gift that could be sterilised. Bundo quickly became a lifeline for Liz, one that she has clung to ever since. Now completely threadbare, shes determined to make him robust enough to face the future.

programme.desc: Former cheerleader Valerie Zavala is invited to a party with high school football players and cheerleaders; she is found dead; investigators collect home video from the party to find clues to identify her killer.

programme.title: Murder Under the Friday Night Lights
programme.desc: When the Broncos draught Texas high school football star Darrent Williams, he stays loyal to his hometown; on New Years, he flies five school friends to Denver for a party; soon after midnight, Darrent dies and no one knows who killed him, or why.

programme.title: Inside The Arrest

programme.title: Faking It: Tears of a Crime

programme.title: Deadliest Families
programme.sub-title: A Mother, A Murder and A Manhunt
programme.desc: A bitter custody battle ends in tragedy when a toddler is found dead. A three-year manhunt ensues to track down her killer, her own mother.

programme.title: Deadliest Families

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Murder on CCTV

programme.title: Wonder Woman
programme.desc: Diana, princess of the Amazons, is raised to be an unconquerable warrior on a sheltered island paradise. She discovers her full powers and true destiny after leaving home to join the fight to end all wars.

programme.title: Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull
programme.desc: Professor Henry Jones is given a coded message during the Cold War which leads him to Peru in search of a legendary skull. Jones needs to deliver the skull to its rightful place, but Soviet agents who are trailing Jones have other plans which will endanger the West if the artefact falls into their hands.

programme.title: Transformers: Dark of the Moon
programme.desc: The Autobots and the Decepticons become involved in a space race to find a hidden Cybertronian spacecraft stranded on the Moon. Sam Witwicky must come to the aid of the Autobots to defeat the evil Decepticons to stop them discovering the secrets of the Cybertronian craft.

programme.title: The Patriot
programme.desc: A widowed farmer, with a brave but brutal military past, decides not to join up when the British arrive in 1776. However, when his son enlists and is later captured by the enemy, the former soldier must abandon his new-found pacifist principles in order to rescue his oldest child, and forms a regiment of Carolina patriots, whose guerrilla tactics prove pivotal to the US war effort.

programme.title: Twisters
programme.desc: Competing teams of storm chasers find themselves in a fight for their lives as multiple systems converge over central Oklahoma.

programme.title: Shazam!

programme.title: Wonder Woman

programme.desc: As humanity picks up the pieces after an epic battle, a shadowy group emerges to gain control of history. Meanwhile, a powerful new menace sets its sights on Earth.

programme.title: Shooter
programme.desc: Following the death of his friend, Bobby decides to leave the army but he is soon asked to come back into action in order to help prevent the assassination of the President.

programme.title: The Dark Tower

Burning rubber all over the city with his ex-con friend, he attempts to deliver the crook to the FBI without crashing and being blown to pieces in one of the many explosions.

programme.title: Fox and Hare Save the Forest
programme.desc: One day, Fox and Hare discover that Owl has disappeared. They go looking for him in the forest with all their friends and discover a big lake that wasnt there before. It is the beginning of an exciting and funny adventure.

programme.title: Rango
programme.desc: An ordinary chameleon, Rango, winds up in the town of Dirt, a lawless outpost in the Wild West in need of a new sheriff.

programme.title: The Ant Bully
programme.desc: This is the story of a ten-year-old boy named Lucas Nickle, who has just moved to a new neighbourhood, has no friends, and is the target for the local bully and his gang. His parents are leaving for a honeymoon in Puerto Vallarta, so they are too busy to attend to his problems. His sister is distracted by her cellular phone, and the grandmother is obsessed with UFOs and aliens.

programme.title: Turbo
programme.desc: A snail gets the chance to escape his slow-paced life and race in the Indianapolis 500 when a freak accident gives him the power of superspeed.

programme.title: The Boss Baby
programme.desc: A wildly imaginative seven-year-old boy discovers that his new brother, Boss Baby, is actually a spy on a secret mission, and only he can help thwart a dastardly plot that involves an epic battle between puppies and babies.

programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., whos intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: The Wild Robot
programme.desc: Shipwrecked on a deserted island, a robot named Roz must learn to adapt to its new surroundings. Building relationships with the native animals, Roz soon develops a parental bond with an orphaned gosling.

programme.title: The Emoji Movie
programme.desc: Three emojis embark on an epic adventure through a smartphone to save their world from being deleted.

programme.title: Penguins of Madagascar
programme.desc: The plucky penguins join a special inter-species task force in an attempt to stop an evil doctor from taking over the world.

programme.title: DC Super Hero Girls: Legends of Atlantis
programme.desc: Siren and Mera steal the powerful Book of Legends. The Super Hero Girls head to Atlantis to recover the book before Siren can use it to create an army and take over the entire ocean.

programme.desc: The Super Hero Girls and the Teen Titans reluctantly band together to fight a new threat to the universe.

programme.title: The Cinema List: Video Games
programme.desc: From pixelated beginnings to the big screen, we bring you your favourite games that made it to the movies - from `Sonic the Hedgehog 3 to `Street Fighter and more.

programme.title: The 40-Year-Old Virgin
programme.desc: Electronics store stock boy Andy Stitzer is horrified when he is found out as a virgin by his workmates - just when he thought he was becoming one of the gang. David, Jay and Cal surprise him by making it their mission to get him a girl and help him lose his virginity.

programme.title: So I Married an Axe Murderer
programme.desc: A lonely poet whos unlucky in love believes hes struck gold when he meets Harriet, an attractive, witty woman who owns a butchers shop. They fall in love and marry, but a series of strange occurrences lead him to suspect that his new wife may be harbouring a dark and deadly secret.

programme.title: Zoolander 2
programme.desc: A special agent recruits former models Derek Zoolander and Hansel to save the worlds most beautiful people from a deadly plot.

programme.title: Instant Family
programme.desc: When Pete and Ellie decide to start a family, they stumble into the world of foster care adoption. They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.title: The Golden Child
programme.desc: A social worker who specialises in missing children is recruited to rescue a mystical Tibetan child from evil forces.

programme.title: White Chicks
programme.desc: Disgraced FBI agents Marcus and Kevin go to bizarre lengths to catch a devious millionaire. They disguise themselves as dumb white socialites Brittany and Tiffany Wilson, the heiresses to a hotel fortune who are under threat. Gross-out comedic farce from the makers of `Scary Movie.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality
programme.desc: An uncouth FBI agent goes undercover in the Miss United States beauty pageant as a contestant to flush out a terrorist who plans to bomb the event. The hard-nosed cop, who prides herself on her toughness, must discard her trainers and feminise her behaviour if she is to avoid blowing her cover.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie is given another makeover to become the face of the Bureau, prior to embarking on a promotional tour. When two of her friends are kidnapped - Miss USA and a beauty pageant director - she decides to investigate on her own, in defiance of orders to the contrary.

programme.title: Instant Family
programme.desc: When Pete and Ellie decide to start a family, they stumble into the world of foster care adoption. They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.title: Semi-Pro
programme.desc: One-hit wonder Jackie Moon uses the profits from his short-lived musical career to buy struggling American Basketball Association team the Flint Tropics and live out his dreams as coach and star player. However, when the ABA decide to merge with the NBA, only four of the ABAs teams can make the move into the more successful league.

programme.title: The Bodyguard
programme.desc: A Secret Service agent-turned-bodyguard finds danger and romance while protecting an entertainer from a stalker.

programme.title: Jungleland
programme.desc: A reluctant bare-knuckle boxer and his manager brother must travel across the country for one last fight, but an unexpected travel companion exposes the cracks in their bond along the way.

programme.title: Two Mules for Sister Sara
programme.desc: Whimsical but engaging western about a drifter who saves a woman from a gang of marauders. The cowboy is astonished to learn that the woman is a nun, especially when he witnesses her heavy drinking and cigar-smoking ways. The unlikely pair embark on a voyage of discovery as they become embroiled in the Mexican War.

programme.title: An Officer and a Gentleman
programme.desc: At a tough training school for American navy pilots, one of the new intake falls foul of the hard-as-nails drill sergeant, but takes it on the chin, and finds the additional strength to help a fellow student who is feeling the pressure.

programme.title: The Damned United
programme.desc: The story of Brian Cloughs 44 day stint as manager of Leeds United Football Club. England, in 1974. When Don Revie quits Leeds to become the England boss, the outspoken Brian Clough takes charge. Determined to impose his own style upon Revies tough tackling team, Clough soon alienates his players and the board. Based on the book by David Pearce.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: Valentines Day
programme.desc: Following a group of Los Angeles residents who try to find their way through the highs and lows of love during a single day.

programme.title: Jerry Maguire
programme.desc: A sports agent who makes the mistake of saying what he believes, finds himself sacked and abandoned by all his clients apart from small time footballer Rod Tidwell. Maguires personal life starts to fall apart as hes torn between love and money in Cameron Crowes metaphor on Americans need for success and money.

programme.title: About a Boy
programme.desc: Parallel coming-of-age stories of a layabout `serial nice guy living a posh, carefree lifestyle off his deceased fathers fortune, and a bright but awkward youth whos tired of his mothers depressed, boyfriend-less state. Their paths collide when the boy fabricates a two-year-old son and joins a group called S.P.A.T. (Single Parents Alone Together).

programme.title: A Star Is Born
programme.desc: Hard-drinking country music star Jackson Maine discovers, and falls in love with, a struggling but talented singer named Ally. As her career quickly takes off, Jackson starts to realize that his best days may be behind him.

programme.title: The Brutalist
programme.desc: Escaping postwar Europe, a visionary architect comes to America to rebuild his life, his career, and his marriage. On his own in a strange new country, he settles in Pennsylvania, where a wealthy and prominent industrialist recognises his talent.

programme.title: The Brutalist
programme.desc: Escaping postwar Europe, a visionary architect comes to America to rebuild his life, his career, and his marriage. On his own in a strange new country, he settles in Pennsylvania, where a wealthy and prominent industrialist recognises his talent.

programme.title: The Cat in the Hat
programme.desc: Humorous adaptation of the classic Dr Seuss childrens book, in which two housebound children are visited by a cat who saves them from their rainy-day boredom by bringing mayhem to their house.

programme.title: Spy Kids
programme.desc: Two siblings set out on a mission to rescue their ex-spy parents. Summoned back to work from their uneventful lives, the adults run into trouble while on duty. When the children are called upon to help them, they find out about their parents former identities while at the same time developing a greater appreciation for each other.

programme.title: Spy Kids 2: The Island of Lost Dreams
programme.desc: The sequel in which the globetrotting sibling duo attempt to reclaim the powerful Transmooker device from the clutches of a mad scientist. Their quest leads them to his tropical island hideaway, which he shares with an assortment of mutant creatures.

programme.title: Spy Kids 3: Game Over
programme.desc: The Spy Kids set off on their third adventure. Secret Agent Carmen finds herself trapped in a 3-D virtual reality video game designed by their new arch nemesis, the Toymaker. Juni must save his sister, as they battle it out against lifelike 3-D monsters, completing each progressively harder level and ultimately trying to save the youth of the world.

programme.title: The Hobbit: An Unexpected Journey
programme.desc: At the urging of the wizard Gandalf, unassuming hobbit Bilbo Baggins joins 13 dwarves on a perilous quest to reclaim the kingdom of Erebor and gains possession of the powerful One Ring.

programme.title: Batman Unlimited: Mechs vs. Mutants
programme.desc: Gotham City faces catastrophe when Mr Freeze transforms Killer Croc and Bane into mega-sized mutant monsters. As the gigantic villains go on the rampage, its up to the caped crusader to cut them down to size.

programme.title: The LEGO Movie
programme.desc: An ordinary LEGO figurine, thought to be the key to saving the world, is accompanied by a fellowship of strangers embarking on a quest to vanquish a tyrant bent on a terrible deed.

programme.title: The LEGO Movie 2: The Second Part
programme.desc: The citizens of Bricksburg face a dangerous new threat when LEGO DUPLO invaders from outer space start to wreck everything in their path. The battle to defeat the enemy and restore harmony to the LEGO universe takes Emmet, Lucy, Batman and the rest of their friends to faraway, unexplored worlds that test their courage and creativity.

programme.title: The LEGO NINJAGO Movie
programme.desc: Master Wu, as wise-cracking as he is wise, leads a fierce team of secret ninja warriors to defeat the evil warlord Garmadon and save Ninjago City.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
programme.desc: Four unlikely warriors rise from the sewers and join forces with fearless reporter April ONeil to save New York from Shredder and his evil minions.

programme.title: E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial
programme.desc: Heartwarming tale in which a ten-year-old boy befriends and shelters a gentle alien creature who has been stranded on Earth. When the authorities are alerted to this unusual house guest, the boy tries to help the creature return home before menacing government agents close in. The film won three Academy Awards for music, sound and special effects.

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop
programme.desc: Paul Blart desperately wants to become a cop, but he is too fat to pass the physical exam. He instead works as a security guard in a shopping mall. One day criminals put the mall under siege, and Blart takes it upon himself to save the day.

programme.title: Paul Blart: Mall Cop 2
programme.desc: Paul Blart takes his teenage daughter with him to Las Vegas for a security-guard expo. While there, he stumbles upon a heist and must single-handedly apprehend the crooks.

programme.title: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Two adopted brothers and rival dancers are thrust together when they are both invited to audition for the World Breaking Championships.

programme.title: Breaking Point
programme.desc: Two adopted brothers and rival dancers are thrust together when they are both invited to audition for the World Breaking Championships.

programme.title: In and Out
programme.desc: The entire town is watching with bated breath as one of their own is up for an Oscar. They get more than they bargain for when he outs his high-school English teacher, who then has to prove to everyone, especially his fiancee, that hes straight. The press turn up and one reporter in particular is making life very difficult.

programme.title: Sky Cinema Preview
programme.desc: Previews of the great films now playing on Sky Cinema, plus other highlights showing on Sky.

programme.title: The Golden Child
programme.desc: A social worker who specialises in missing children is recruited to rescue a mystical Tibetan child from evil forces.

programme.title: Shirley Valentine
programme.desc: Shirley is a Liverpudlian housewife stuck in a loveless marriage. One day she finds herself talking to the wall, and decides to make some changes in her life. Flying off to the Greek island of Mykonos on her own, she is gently romanced by middle-aged waiter Costas.

programme.title: Dumb and Dumber
programme.desc: A pair of idiotic friends set off on a madcap journey to return a briefcase to its owner. However, they are blissfully unaware that the case contains a large amount of ransom money for the owners kidnapped husband. Their trip is soon fraught with disaster, especially when the kidnappers begin to pursue them across the country with the aim of grabbing the cash.

programme.title: Big Daddy
programme.desc: A thirtysomething slacker attempts to inject some stability into his life by adopting an impressionable five-year-old.

programme.title: The Blues Brothers
programme.desc: The story of a pair of blues musicians who try to raise the money to save an orphanage by reuniting their old band - nearly writing off Chicago in the process.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: Twister
programme.desc: Weather scientists Jo and Bill put marital problems aside to track a slew of devastating tornadoes raging through Oklahoma and stay one step ahead of their better-equipped but arrogant rivals. They have to get to the tornado first to be able to test a radical new device, by inserting it directly into the heart of the storm.

programme.title: Jaws
programme.desc: Scary chiller about a coastal community plagued by a killer shark. With the summer tourist season at hand, the mayor is keen to keep the first attack hushed up. However, when an expert claims that the attacks are the work of a giant great white shark, there is only one solution - to kill the monster fish before it can feed again.

programme.title: Jaws 2
programme.desc: Another great white shark arrives to terrorise the holiday resort of Amity Island. Having recovered from the grisly events of four years ago, the island is once again packed with tourists. Families are sailing in the bay and swimming off the beach, but when two divers and a water-skier go missing, the local police chief fears that he is about to relive his greatest nightmare.

programme.title: Jaws III
programme.desc: Second sequel to Steven Spielbergs 1970s blockbuster transports the action from Amity Island to a new undersea kingdom attraction in a Florida theme park. The capture of a baby great white shark is big news and brings crowds to the park - until its mother arrives on the scene.

programme.title: Pet Sematary
programme.desc: When a familys pet cat is called, they manage to resurrect the animal by placing it in an Indian burial ground which, it is believed, can revive the dead, although in doing so they create a monster.

programme.title: The Exorcist
programme.desc: A famous actress calls upon medical science for help when her daughter begins behaving strangely, but without any success. Then, as bizarre and horrific incidents involving the daughter increase, her mother turns in desperation to the Church, and to a priest who doubts his own faith.

programme.title: The First Wives Club

Vowing revenge, they decide to hit them where it hurts most: the pocket. Using help and their various skills, they begin to pick them off one by on.

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring
programme.desc: Frodo is a hobbit who elects to become the bearer of the Ring of Power, which, if reclaimed by its maker and master Lord Sauron, will see him gain full and terrible power over all of Middle Earth. Frodo sets out with The Fellowship of the Ring, led by Gandalf and Aragorn, and six other companions to Saurons stronghold, Mordor, the only place where the ring can be destroyed.

programme.title: The Lord of the Rings: The Two Towers
programme.desc: Hobbits Frodo and Sam discover they are being followed by the mysterious Gollum. A mercurial creature who has been warped by the Ring, Gollum promises to guide the Hobbits to the Black Gates of Mordor if they will release him. Middle-earth Aragorn, the Elf archer Legolas and Gimli the Dwarf encounter the besieged Rohan kingdom, whose once great King Theoden has fallen under Sarumans deadly spell.

programme.desc: Learn how cockatoos and platypuses inspired new careers, then explore the shared trauma that bonded a human and an elephant for life. Get playful with 35-year-old George the turkey vulture and say a moving farewell to human-raised kangaroos.

programme.title: A Bees Diary
programme.desc: Bees are one of the most important species on the planet. Follow the trials and tribulations of two particular honeybees over two years from birth to death.

programme.title: Wild Tales From The Farm
programme.desc: Spring is in the air and life on the farm is bursting out everywhere, as twin lambs Larry and Flora leap for joy in the meadow with the other newborns.

programme.title: Arabian Seas
programme.desc: The seas of Arabia are alive with carnivores. Sharks and rays patrol the coral reefs hunting down their prey. Toxic fish disguise themselves to ambush the unwary and bizarre armoured predators devour the vulnerable. These are the Carnivores of Arabias Coral Gardens.

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: There are clashes over precious resources when the dry season brings a band of gladiators to an African savannah watering hole. There is a lion-elephant territory brawl, an ant raid on a termite mound and a honey badgers dance with a deadly snake.

programme.title: Nature
programme.desc: Capturing the overlooked life of the elephant and the irreplaceable role he plays in the survival of his species.

programme.title: Colombias Wild Coast
programme.desc: Explore Colombias wild coast, a natural fortress surrounded by shark-infested seas, blanketed in impenetrable jungle and teaming with deadly snakes.

programme.title: Colombias Wild Coast
programme.desc: A young female black crab is about to undertake the biggest mission of her life - to escort her 80,000 eggs from her mountain top home.

programme.title: Volcanic Odysseys
programme.desc: Volcanic folklore is reflected in Mexican history. One of their ancient legends tells the story of the Aztec warrior Popocatepetl - a giant active volcano - who erupts to wake his sleeping princess Iztaccíhuatl, a neighbouring extinct ice capped volcano.

programme.title: Oceans Greatest Feast
programme.desc: Get a front-row seat to the planets biggest biomass migration, as billions of sardines migrate along South Africas east coast, each hoping to escape the jaws of hungry predators.

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a birds-eye view in this spectacular documentary, charting the king penguins attempts to survive, find love and raise a family in the unforgiving Antarctic.

programme.title: Micro Monsters With David Attenborough
programme.desc: Its a bug-eating world out there, and the documentary continues with a look at how the various critters disable the defences of their respective prey.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: Feel the heat when the pride tries to hunt down adequate food as drought conditions push them (and their prey) dangerously close to death.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: Welcome to the shores Canadas Hudson Bay, where a unique community polar bears are battling to survive in a rapidly changing landscape.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: From the dry plains of the interior come the most recognizable images of Australia, the monolithic red rock of Uluru, the languid, bounding Red Kangaroo. The Arid Heart of Australia is vast and iconic.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: Created by titanic forces, the Great Dividing Range separates a thin ribbon of green from the continents arid heart.

programme.title: Pride Rules
programme.desc: Feel the heat when the pride tries to hunt down adequate food as drought conditions push them (and their prey) dangerously close to death.

programme.title: Predators
programme.desc: Welcome to the shores Canadas Hudson Bay, where a unique community polar bears are battling to survive in a rapidly changing landscape.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kante and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Ancient Enemies: In Africa, lions and wolves lead their ecosystems. Discover the strength, social strategies and hierarchies behind how they raise and protect their young.

programme.title: Animal Senses
programme.desc: The first sense to evolve in the first animals was chemoreception. It means being able to detect chemicals in the animals environment. This helps animals find prey and mates, and to sense predators from a distance.

programme.title: Animal Senses
programme.desc: The sense of touch is useful to feel physical forces such as bites, changes in temperature and texture. Some animals have become extra sensitive to feel very faint vibrations, which give them a lot of information about their environment.

programme.title: Animal Senses
programme.desc: Seeing the world is one of the most amazing animal adaptations. Different types of eyes give animals different types of vision, and in some cases they can be extraordinary.

programme.title: Animal Senses
programme.desc: Sound waves are produced by movement. Animals have evolved organs to detect those sound waves in the air or water to perceive movements in their environment. This can be used to find prey, predators, and mates. These are some of the animals that have mastered the sense of hearing.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: Near the Snowy Mountains of southeast Australia is Wombat Wood, a wooded area dotted with the burrows of wombats.

programme.title: Secret Life of the Wombat
programme.desc: The breeding season brings out wombats energy, aggression and playfulness.

programme.title: Survival of the Beast
programme.desc: Max is on a mission to unlock and mimic the survival skills of some animals.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: From the dry plains of the interior come the most recognizable images of Australia, the monolithic red rock of Uluru, the languid, bounding Red Kangaroo. The Arid Heart of Australia is vast and iconic.

programme.title: Over Australia
programme.desc: Created by titanic forces, the Great Dividing Range separates a thin ribbon of green from the continents arid heart.

programme.title: Extreme Africa
programme.desc: South Africas Drakensberg is one of the continents most impressive landscapes. It is home to sharp climatic and ecological gradients as physical conditions change for both land-based creatures and the Raptors that swirl in the skies above.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Novice fliers tackle their first ascents, earth-bound creatures soar uniquely and sky masters race with speed, power and agility.

programme.title: Whale with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores the evolving understanding of whales, how much they have in common with humans, and how recent discoveries could help to better protect them from threats in an increasingly changing world.

programme.title: Whale with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve encounters the largest and most sophisticated hunters on the planet, revealing how the ingenious strategies used by whales can sometimes get these extraordinary predators in trouble.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kante and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Ancient Enemies: In Africa, lions and wolves lead their ecosystems. Discover the strength, social strategies and hierarchies behind how they raise and protect their young.

programme.title: David Attenboroughs First Life
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough journeys across the globe to discover how the earliest animals developed on our planet. Attenborough meets expert fossil hunters, and sees on-site some of the most remarkable finds that have transformed our understanding of the first animal life forms, including the first set of eyes and teeth and the first predators and parasites.

programme.title: David Attenboroughs First Life
programme.desc: Sir David Attenboroughs journey continues in Canadas Rocky Mountains, where fossils document an explosion in animal diversity never seen before or since. Travelling on to North Africa, the rainforests of Australia and the east coast of Scotland, Attenborough discovers how animals evolved to conquer not only the oceans but also the land and air.

programme.title: The Bachelor King
programme.desc: David Attenborough takes a birds-eye view in this spectacular documentary, charting the king penguins attempts to survive, find love and raise a family in the unforgiving Antarctic.

programme.title: Humanlike: The Chimps of Fongoli
programme.desc: Join anthropologist Jill Pruetz and guide Dondo (Johnny) Kante and watch evolution happen in real time. Meet the elusive chimps of Fongoli, Senegal, who use tools, interact with fire, and adapt to the human world as it collides with their own.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Ancient Enemies: In Africa, lions and wolves lead their ecosystems. Discover the strength, social strategies and hierarchies behind how they raise and protect their young.

programme.title: Wild Canary Islands
programme.desc: When rocks engulfed by flames emerge from the middle of the ocean, new opportunities arise for the species that manage to reach them... This is the case of the Canary Islands, eight islands born from fire, that form an archipelago full of life.

programme.title: Valley of Flood and Fire
programme.desc: Its the end of November in the Luangwa Valley, and the rains have just begun. It marks the end of one of the toughest dry season on record, and triggers a transition from parched wilderness to luscious wetland.

programme.title: Valley of Flood and Fire
programme.desc: Its the end of November in the Luangwa Valley, and the rains have just begun. It marks the end of one of the toughest dry season on record, and triggers a transition from parched wilderness to luscious wetland.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When Stephen Hawking comes to lecture at the university for a week, Wolowitz is given the task of maintaining his wheelchair, making Sheldon green with envy. When Sheldon begs Wolowitz to introduce him to his hero Hawking, Wolowitz takes full advantage and only agrees after making Sheldon carry out a series of humiliating tasks, including doing his laundry, cleaning his room, and taking his mother dress shopping.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard, Sheldon and Koothrappali throw a classy bachelor party for Wolowitz, during which some stories about the future bridegrooms colourful sexual history are accidentally revealed. When Bernadette hears about it, she becomes upset and threatens to call off the wedding. Can Wolowitz win her over with a convincing explanation and a heartfelt apology to get the nuptials back on track?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When NASA tells a nervous Wolowitz that his space mission has been rescheduled, he tries to use the wedding as an excuse not to go, but hes forced to agree when he realises that Bernadettes father only likes him because hes an astronaut. Amy develops a cunning psychological plan to bring Sheldon closer, and Penny is surprised by a proposal at an inappropriate moment.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: In the last episode of season five, Bernadette decides that she wants to be married to Wolowitz before his NASA space mission takes place. They scramble to organise a wedding ceremony at City Hall in the short time available, but their plans go awry. Can their friends step in to find an ordained minister and a romantic wedding venue before its too late?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard gets caught in an argument between his mum and Bernadette while at the International Space Station. Meanwhile, Ray is feeling lonely so Sheldon invites him along to his second anniversary date with Amy.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon intervenes when he learns that Penny is thinking about breaking up with Leonard, but he is hampered by his inability to keep a secret. Meanwhile, in space, Howard is being picked on by the other astronauts.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy is jealous when Sheldon hires an attractive female assistant named Alex. However, it turns out that Alex is more interested in Leonard, which makes Penny feel threatened. Meanwhile, Howard discovers that his return from space has been delayed by a week and the pressure begins to take its toll on his mind.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Wolowitz is disappointed when he doesnt receive a heros welcome on his return from space. Bernadette is out of action with a heavy cold, his mother is occupied with her dentist, Koothrappali has a new best friend, and Sheldon, Amy, Leonard and Penny are deeply involved in an ultra-competitive games night. Will Wolowitz ever get the recognition he deserves as an astronaut?

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The gang celebrates Halloween with a fancy dress party at Stuarts comic book shop, but Sheldon and Amy cant agree about which costumes to wear. Leonard and Penny get a new spark in their relationship when she visits him at his laboratory. Howard just cant stop talking about his trip to space, much to the irritation of his friends, and it takes a word from astronaut Buzz Aldrin to get his feet back on the ground.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon gets excited when Stephen Hawking agrees to play against him in an online game of `Words with Friends. However, when Sheldon starts to dominate the game, Hawking is less than pleased. Elsewhere, Penny signs up for a class at community college but feels reluctant to tell Leonard about it.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: The guys notice that Sheldon disappears for 20 minutes right after lunch every day. Filled with curiosity, they attempt to follow him and work out what on earth hes been doing. Meanwhile, Penny befriends a British student in her class and they start exchanging text messages, which makes Leonard jealous, so he tries to discourage their friendship.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: When non-driver Sheldon finds out that his unused parking space at the university has been reallocated to Howard he feels undermined. Howard doesnt see the problem in using something that his friend doesnt need and the two fall out. As their disagreement escalates, each begins to steal items that are not being used at the time from the other, while Amy and Bernadette gradually get drawn into the dispute.

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
